description_long: This object is a fragmented marble stele or obelisk, featuring a rectangular upper section with a relief carving depicting several human figures in classical attire. The relief is bordered by a simple frame, and the upper right corner is broken, revealing the object's age and wear. The lower portion of the obelisk is irregular and incomplete, with visible breaks and missing sections, supported by metal rods attached to a rectangular stone base. The marble surface is predominantly white with some discoloration and weathering, and the relief is rendered in a classical Greco-Roman style. The base is plain and functional, designed to stabilize the upright fragment for display or preservation.
description: A fragmented marble obelisk with a classical relief of human figures, mounted on a plain stone base and supported by metal rods.
description_view_0: The front view shows the detailed relief carving of human figures and the broken upper right corner, with the irregular lower section and supporting rods clearly visible.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the thinness of the obelisk and the depth of the relief, as well as the extent of the breakage along the side.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the rough, unfinished back of the obelisk, with large areas of missing marble and visible support rods.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the thickness of the marble and the plain, undecorated edge, along with the supporting base.
attribute_ornamentation: A relief carving of several human figures in classical attire is present on the upper front face, rendered in a Greco-Roman style.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple rectangular stone slab, unadorned, with metal rods providing structural support for the fragmented obelisk.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is broken and irregular, with no decorative features or pointed design remaining.
